data.title: Long-lived cache headers for embedded static assets

data.body:
Severity: performance low. Source: TYB-12-followup performance audit.

Where: server/internal/httpserver/router.go:18-19, 47.

What: http.FileServer(http.FS(publicFS)) serves all static assets with no Cache-Control, no ETag, and Last-Modified set to the embed.FS zero time (effectively useless for caching). Helvetica-Black.woff (~29 KB) and .woff2 (~23 KB) reload on every page navigation. Already-compressed assets (PNG, WOFF2) shouldn't be gzipped; HTML/CSS/JS arguably should be.

Impact: cold visitor pays an extra ~140 KB of asset roundtrips per refresh. Not a bottleneck but a free win for any visitor that returns or refreshes.

Recommendation: wrap staticHandler with a small middleware that sets Cache-Control headers based on extension:
- *.woff, *.woff2, *.png, *.ico → 'public, max-age=31536000, immutable' (1 year)
- *.html, *.css, *.js → 'public, max-age=300' (5 min — short enough to push fast updates)
Optional: add an ETag based on a content hash computed once at boot from publicFS.

Effort: small (~30 LoC). No new deps.
